response:
Here is a summary of the text in 3-4 sentences:

The European Union's General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), which took effect in May 2018, has significantly impacted how organizations handle personal data, applying to any company processing EU residents' data. Key provisions include the right to access and erase personal data, data portability, and explicit consent, with fines for non-compliance reaching up to 4% of global annual revenue or 20 million euros. Major tech companies, including Meta, Amazon, and Google, have received significant fines for violating GDPR, with totals ranging from 200 million to 1.2 billion euros. Despite criticisms of compliance burdens and inconsistent enforcement, GDPR has set a global standard for data protection legislation, inspiring similar laws in California, Brazil, and India.
